square_shape_match_label: Which panel shows four points that are vertices of a square?
Choose the panel whose four points form a square.
Which labeled panel contains four vertices of a square?
Find the panel where the four plotted points make a square.
Which panel's four points form a square?
rectangle_shape_match_label: Which panel shows four points that are vertices of a rectangle?
Choose the panel whose four points form a rectangle.
Which labeled panel contains four vertices of a rectangle?
Find the panel where the four plotted points make a rectangle.
Which panel's four points form a rectangle?
rhombus_shape_match_label: Which panel shows four points that are vertices of a rhombus?
Choose the panel whose four points form a rhombus.
Which labeled panel contains four vertices of a rhombus?
Find the panel where the four plotted points make a rhombus.
Which panel's four points form a rhombus?
parallelogram_shape_match_label: Which panel shows four points that are vertices of a parallelogram?
Choose the panel whose four points form a parallelogram.
Which labeled panel contains four vertices of a parallelogram?
Find the panel where the four plotted points make a parallelogram.
Which panel's four points form a parallelogram?
parallel_segments_match_label: Which panel shows two parallel line segments?
Choose the panel where the two colored segments are parallel.
Which labeled panel contains a parallel pair of segments?
Find the panel whose two line segments are parallel.
Which panel has two segments with the same direction?
perpendicular_segments_match_label: Which panel shows two perpendicular line segments?
Choose the panel where the two colored segments are perpendicular.
Which labeled panel contains a perpendicular pair of segments?
Find the panel whose two line segments meet at right angles if extended.
Which panel has two segments with perpendicular directions?
equal_length_segments_match_label: Which panel shows two line segments with equal length?
Choose the panel where the two colored segments are the same length.
Which labeled panel contains an equal-length pair of segments?
Find the panel whose two line segments have equal length.
Which panel has two segments of matching length?
translation_match_label: Which panel shows the candidate point set as a translation of the source point set?
Choose the panel where the candidate points are a translated copy of the source points.
Which labeled panel contains a source point set and its translated candidate point set?
Find the panel where the candidate set is made by translating the source set.
Which panel shows a translation from the source points to the candidate points?
reflection_x_match_label: Which panel shows the candidate point set as a reflection of the source point set across the x-axis?
Choose the panel where the candidate points reflect the source points across the x-axis.
Which labeled panel contains a source point set and its x-axis reflection?
Find the panel where the candidate set is made by reflecting the source set across the x-axis.
Which panel shows reflection across the x-axis from source points to candidate points?
reflection_y_match_label: Which panel shows the candidate point set as a reflection of the source point set across the y-axis?
Choose the panel where the candidate points reflect the source points across the y-axis.
Which labeled panel contains a source point set and its y-axis reflection?
Find the panel where the candidate set is made by reflecting the source set across the y-axis.
Which panel shows reflection across the y-axis from source points to candidate points?
rotation_180_match_label: Which panel shows the candidate point set as a 180-degree rotation of the source point set about the origin?
Choose the panel where the candidate points are the source points rotated 180 degrees about the origin.
Which labeled panel contains a source point set and its 180-degree rotation about the origin?
Find the panel where the candidate set is made by rotating the source set 180 degrees about the origin.
Which panel shows a 180-degree rotation from source points to candidate points?

scene:coordinate_panels_scene.object_description: labeled coordinate panels, each containing four unlabeled points
scene:coordinate_panels_scene.object_description_segment_relation: labeled coordinate panels, each containing two colored line segments
scene:coordinate_panels_scene.object_description_point_set_transform: labeled coordinate panels, each containing a source point set and a candidate point set

answer_or_annotation:answer_only.answer_hint_option_letter: set "answer" to the selected panel label as a capital letter
answer_or_annotation:answer_only.answer_hint: set "answer" to the selected panel label as a capital letter

answer_or_annotation:answer_and_annotati.annotation_hint_selected_panel_point_set: set "annotation" to an array of the four pixel points [x,y] plotted inside the selected panel
answer_or_annotation:answer_and_annotati.annotation_hint_selected_panel_segment_s: set "annotation" to an array of the two selected-panel pixel segments, each segment as [[x0,y0],[x1,y1]]
answer_or_annotation:answer_and_annotati.annotation_hint_selected_panel_transform: set "annotation" to an array of the source and candidate pixel points [x,y] plotted inside the selected panel
answer_or_annotation:answer_and_annotati.answer_hint_option_letter: set "answer" to the selected panel label as a capital letter
answer_or_annotation:answer_and_annotati.answer_hint: set "answer" to the selected panel label as a capital letter
answer_or_annotation:answer_and_annotati.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to an array of the four pixel points [x,y] plotted inside the selected panel
